scale.tool_name: ARISCAT Score for Postoperative Pulmonary Complications
scale.description: The ARISCAT score is a tool used to assess the risk of postoperative pulmonary complications (PPCs) in patients undergoing surgery. It evaluates seven preoperative factors, including age, preoperative oxygen saturation, respiratory infection in the last month, anemia, and type of surgery, to predict the likelihood of PPCs. This scoring system helps healthcare providers identify high-risk patients, enabling targeted preventive measures to reduce complications.

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: Patient's age in years

parameters.name: spo2
parameters.description: Preoperative oxygen saturation (SpO2) percentage

parameters.name: respiratory_infection
parameters.description: Indicates the presence of respiratory infection in the last month. 0 for 'No', 1 for 'Yes'

parameters.name: anemia
parameters.description: Indicates preoperative anemia (Hgb ≤10 g/dL). 0 for 'No', 1 for 'Yes'

parameters.name: surgical_incision
parameters.description: Type of surgical incision. 0 for 'Peripheral', 1 for 'Upper abdominal', 2 for 'Intrathoracic'

parameters.name: surgery_duration
parameters.description: Duration of the surgery in hours

parameters.name: emergency
parameters.description: Indicates if the procedure was an emergency. 0 for 'No', 1 for 'Yes'

returns.description: The ARISCAT score, which is a summation of points based on the provided parameters

scale.description: The Morphine Milligram Equivalents (MME) Calculator is used to convert various opioid dosages into a standardized morphine equivalent to help assess opioid use risk and ensure safe dosing. It is particularly useful in clinical settings where healthcare providers manage patients with chronic pain or are prescribing multiple opioids. The tool aids in maintaining dosage safety and compliance with guidelines to minimize the risk of opioid misuse and overdose.

parameters.name: dosages
parameters.description: List of dosages for each drug in the units appropriate for the drug

parameters.name: doses_per_day
parameters.description: List of doses per day for each corresponding drug

parameters.name: drugs
parameters.description: List of indexes representing drugs where each index corresponds to:

returns.description: Total MME/day calculated across all provided drugs

scale.description: The Centor Score, also known as the Modified Centor Score or McIsaac Score, is a clinical tool used to assess the likelihood of streptococcal pharyngitis in patients presenting with a sore throat. It evaluates factors such as age, fever, tonsillar exudates, tender anterior cervical lymphadenopathy, and absence of cough to guide clinicians on whether to perform further testing or prescribe antibiotics. This scoring system helps reduce unnecessary antibiotic use by identifying patients who are most likely to benefit from streptococcal testing.

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: Age of the patient in years

parameters.name: exudate_swelling
parameters.description: True if there is exudate or swelling on the tonsils, False otherwise

parameters.name: tender_swollen_lymph_nodes
parameters.description: True if there are tender/swollen anterior cervical lymph nodes, False otherwise

parameters.name: temperature
parameters.description: Patient's temperature in degrees Celsius

parameters.name: cough
parameters.description: True if cough is present, False otherwise

returns.description: The calculated Centor Score, which ranges from -1 to 4

scale.description: The Free Water Deficit (FWD) calculation is used in the management of hypernatremia to estimate the amount of water required to normalize a patient's serum sodium concentration. It is particularly useful in clinical settings for patients with elevated sodium levels due to water loss or low water intake. This calculation helps guide appropriate fluid replacement strategies, ensuring both the safety and effectiveness of treatment.

parameters.name: gender_age_group
parameters.description: Index representing the patient's gender and age group

parameters.name: weight_kg
parameters.description: The weight of the patient in kilograms

parameters.name: current_na
parameters.description: The current serum sodium level of the patient (mmol/L)

parameters.name: ideal_na
parameters.description: The target or ideal serum sodium level for the patient (mmol/L)

returns.description: The calculated free water deficit in liters

scale.description: Wells' Criteria for Pulmonary Embolism is a clinical tool used to assess the probability of pulmonary embolism (PE) in patients presenting with symptoms suggestive of this condition. It involves scoring various clinical factors and symptoms to categorize the likelihood of PE as low, moderate, or high. This tool guides clinicians in deciding whether further diagnostic testing, such as a D-dimer test or imaging, is necessary.

parameters.name: dvt_symptoms
parameters.description: True if there are clinical signs and symptoms of deep vein thrombosis (DVT), else False

parameters.name: pe_diagnosis
parameters.description: True if pulmonary embolism is the #1 diagnosis or equally likely, else False

parameters.name: heart_rate
parameters.description: True if heart rate is over 100, else False

parameters.name: immobilization_surgery
parameters.description: True if patient has been immobilized for at least 3 days or had surgery in the past 4 weeks, else False

parameters.name: previous_pe_dvt
parameters.description: True if there is a history of previously diagnosed PE or DVT, else False

parameters.name: hemoptysis
parameters.description: True if the patient has hemoptysis, else False

parameters.name: malignancy
parameters.description: True if the patient has malignancy with treatment within the last 6 months or is on palliative, else False

returns.description: The total score based on Wells' Criteria, which can be used to estimate the risk of pulmonary embolism

scale.description: The Anion Gap is a diagnostic tool used to help identify disturbances in acid-base balance in the body, typically by calculating the difference between primary measured cations and anions in serum. It is particularly useful in clinical settings for diagnosing the causes of metabolic acidosis, such as renal failure, diabetic ketoacidosis, or poisoning. Physicians often use this calculation when a patient presents with altered mental status, unexplained nausea, fatigue, or breathing difficulties.

parameters.name: sodium
parameters.description: Sodium level in mEq/L

parameters.name: chloride
parameters.description: Chloride level in mEq/L

parameters.name: bicarbonate
parameters.description: Bicarbonate level in mEq/L

returns.description: The calculated Anion Gap in mEq/L

scale.description: The PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire-9) is a widely used self-administered diagnostic tool for assessing the presence and severity of depression. It consists of nine questions that align with the diagnostic criteria for depression from the DSM-IV (Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders). Clinicians typically use the PHQ-9 in both medical and mental health settings to screen for depression during routine health assessments and to monitor treatment progress.

parameters.name: interest_pleasure
parameters.description: Index from 0 to 3 indicating frequency of little interest or pleasure in doing things

parameters.name: feeling_down
parameters.description: Index from 0 to 3 indicating frequency of feeling down, depressed, or hopeless

parameters.name: sleep_issues
parameters.description: Index from 0 to 3 indicating trouble with falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much

parameters.name: tired_energy
parameters.description: Index from 0 to 3 indicating feeling tired or having little energy

parameters.name: appetite_issues
parameters.description: Index from 0 to 3 indicating issues with poor appetite or overeating

parameters.name: self_feeling
parameters.description: Index from 0 to 3 indicating feelings of being bad about oneself, or feeling like a failure

parameters.name: concentration
parameters.description: Index from 0 to 3 indicating trouble concentrating on things

parameters.name: activity_level
parameters.description: Index from 0 to 3 indicating altered activity levels, either moving too slowly or being too fidgety

parameters.name: suicidal_thoughts
parameters.description: Index from 0 to 3 indicating frequency of thoughts of self-harm or being better off dead

returns.description: The total PHQ-9 score, which is the sum of all the individual scores for the symptoms listed above

scale.description: The GAD-7 (General Anxiety Disorder-7) is a self-administered questionnaire used to screen for and measure the severity of generalized anxiety disorder. It consists of seven questions that evaluate the frequency of anxiety symptoms over the past two weeks. This tool is widely used in clinical settings and research to facilitate the diagnosis and monitoring of anxiety.

parameters.name: nervous
parameters.description: Score for feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge

parameters.name: control_worrying
parameters.description: Score for not being able to stop or control worrying

parameters.name: worry_too_much
parameters.description: Score for worrying too much about different things

parameters.name: trouble_relaxing
parameters.description: Score for having trouble relaxing

parameters.name: restlessness
parameters.description: Score for being so restless that it is hard to sit still

parameters.name: irritability
parameters.description: Score for becoming easily annoyed or irritable

parameters.name: fear_of_awful_events
parameters.description: Score for feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

returns.description: The total GAD-7 score, ranging from 0 to 21

scale.description: The Clinical Institute Withdrawal Assessment for Alcohol, Revised (CIWA-Ar) is a tool used to assess the severity of alcohol withdrawal symptoms and guide treatment decisions. It is commonly used in hospital settings, particularly in emergency departments and detoxification units, to monitor withdrawal progression and determine the need for medication. The CIWA-Ar evaluates symptoms such as tremors, agitation, and hallucinations, facilitating targeted and safe management of withdrawal.

parameters.name: nausea_vomiting
parameters.description: Index for nausea and vomiting severity. Range: 0-7

parameters.name: tremor
parameters.description: Index for tremor severity. Range: 0-7

parameters.name: paroxysmal_sweats
parameters.description: Index for sweating severity. Range: 0-7

parameters.name: anxiety
parameters.description: Index for anxiety level. Range: 0-7

parameters.name: agitation
parameters.description: Index for agitation level. Range: 0-7

parameters.name: tactile_disturbances
parameters.description: Index for tactile disturbances. Range: 0-7

parameters.name: visual_disturbances
parameters.description: Index for visual disturbances. Range: 0-7

parameters.name: headache_fullness
parameters.description: Index for headache or fullness in head. Range: 0-7

parameters.name: orientation_sensorium
parameters.description: Index for orientation or clouding of sensorium. Range: 0-4

returns.description: The total CIWA-Ar score, representing the severity of alcohol withdrawal

scale.tool_name: Revised Cardiac Risk Index for Pre-Operative Risk
scale.description: The Revised Cardiac Risk Index (RCRI) is a clinical tool used to estimate the risk of major cardiac complications, like heart attacks or cardiac arrest, in patients undergoing non-cardiac surgery. It evaluates risk based on six pre-operative criteria, including history of ischemic heart disease, congestive heart failure, and insulin therapy for diabetes, among others. This index aids healthcare providers in identifying high-risk patients who may benefit from further cardiac evaluation or specific perioperative management strategies.

parameters.name: elevated_risk_surgery
parameters.description: True if the surgery is intraperitoneal, intrathoracic, or suprainguinal vascular

parameters.name: history_of_ischemic_heart_disease
parameters.description: True if there is a history of myocardial infarction, positive exercise test, current chest pain considered due to myocardial ischemia, or use of nitrate therapy or ECG showing pathological Q waves

parameters.name: history_of_congestive_heart_failure
parameters.description: True if there are signs such as pulmonary edema, bilateral rales or S3 gallop, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, or chest X-ray showing pulmonary vascular redistribution

parameters.name: history_of_cerebrovascular_disease
parameters.description: True if there is a history of transient ischemic attack or stroke

parameters.name: preop_treatment_with_insulin
parameters.description: True if the patient is being treated with insulin pre-operatively

parameters.name: preop_creatinine_above_threshold
parameters.description: True if pre-operative creatinine levels are greater than 2 mg/dL or 176.8 µmol/L

returns.description: The total Revised Cardiac Risk Index score, which is the sum of the individual risk factors present

scale.tool_name: HEART Score for Major Cardiac Events
scale.description: The HEART Score for Major Cardiac Events is a clinical tool used to assess the risk of major adverse cardiac events in patients presenting with chest pain. It evaluates five components: History, ECG, Age, Risk factors, and Troponin levels, assigning points to each category to stratify patients into low, medium, or high risk. This score aids clinicians in emergency departments or outpatient settings in deciding on patient management strategies, including discharge, further observation, or urgent intervention.

returns.description: The total HEART score, which can guide further management of chest pain patients

scale.description: The APACHE II Score is a severity-of-disease classification system used in intensive care units(ICU) to predict severity and hospital mortality for critically ill patients. It evaluates 12 routine physiological measurements, patient age, previous health status, and type of admission to generate a score. This score is used to calculate the patient's expected mortality rate, aiding in treatment decisions and resource allocation.

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: Age of the patient in years

parameters.name: temperature
parameters.description: Rectal temperature in degrees Celsius

parameters.name: mean_arterial_pressure
parameters.description: Mean arterial pressure in mm Hg

parameters.name: heart_rate
parameters.description: Heart rate in beats per minute

parameters.name: respiratory_rate
parameters.description: Respiratory rate in breaths per minute

parameters.name: sodium
parameters.description: Serum sodium in mmol/L

parameters.name: potassium
parameters.description: Serum potassium in mmol/L

parameters.name: creatinine
parameters.description: Serum creatinine in mg/100 mL

parameters.name: hematocrit
parameters.description: Hematocrit percentage

parameters.name: white_blood_cell_count
parameters.description: White blood cell count per cubic millimeter

parameters.name: gcs
parameters.description: Glasgow Coma Scale score, ranging from 3 (most impaired) to 15 (fully awake)

parameters.name: ph
parameters.description: Arterial pH

parameters.name: history_of_severe_organ_insufficiency
parameters.description: Indicates the patient's history status, with 0 for 'No', 1 for 'Yes, and elective postoperative patient', and 2 for 'Yes, and nonoperative or emergency postoperative patient'

parameters.name: acute_renal_failure
parameters.description: Indicates if the patient has acute renal failure, with 0 for 'No' and 1 for 'Yes'

parameters.name: fio2
parameters.description: Fraction of inspired oxygen, 0 for '<50%' and 1 for '≥50%'

parameters.name: pao2
parameters.description: Partial pressure of arterial oxygen, required if FiO2 < 50%

parameters.name: a_a_gradient
parameters.description: Alveolar-arterial gradient, required if FiO2 ≥ 50%

returns.description: The computed APACHE II score

scale.description: The Fibrosis-4 (FIB-4) Index is a clinical tool used to estimate the level of scarring, or fibrosis, in the liver, particularly helpful in assessing liver damage in patients with chronic liver diseases like hepatitis C and NAFLD. It calculates scores based on age, and blood tests results—namely, platelet count, aspartate aminotransferase (AST), and alanine aminotransferase (ALT). FIB-4 is widely used in both clinical and outpatient settings as a non-invasive, cost-effective alternative to liver biopsy, helping to identify patients who may require further hepatological assessment or monitoring.

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: Age of the patient in years

parameters.name: ast
parameters.description: Aspartate aminotransferase level in U/L

parameters.name: platelets
parameters.description: Platelet count is measured in units of × 10⁹ per liter (× 10⁹ / L)

parameters.name: alt
parameters.description: Alanine aminotransferase level in U/L

returns.description: The FIB-4 Index score, which can be used to categorize liver fibrosis into mild,

scale.tool_name: Body Mass Index (BMI) and Body Surface Area (BSA)
scale.description: The Body Mass Index (BMI) is a simple calculation used to assess whether an individual has a healthy body weight for a person of their height, widely used in general health assessments and public health studies. Body Surface Area (BSA) is used to calculate medication dosages and medical indicators, such as renal function, more accurately based on the physical body area of an individual. While BMI is used for general evaluations of weight-related health risks, BSA is more specific and is often applied in clinical settings to tailor treatments to individual patient needs.

parameters.name: weight_kg
parameters.description: Weight of the individual in kilograms

parameters.name: height_cm
parameters.description: Height of the individual in centimeters

returns.description: Dictionary containing 'BMI' and 'BSA' with their respective values

scale.description: Calcium correction for hypoalbuminemia is used to accurately assess the level of bioavailable calcium in patients with low albumin levels, which can distort true calcium readings. This correction is particularly important in clinical settings such as hospitals and clinics where patients may present with malnutrition, liver disease, or other conditions affecting albumin production. The corrected calcium formula helps guide appropriate treatment decisions, ensuring patient safety and effective management of calcium-related disorders.

returns.description: The corrected serum calcium level in mg/dL

scale.tool_name: HOMA-IR (Homeostatic Model Assessment for Insulin Resistance)
scale.description: The HOMA-IR (Homeostatic Model Assessment for Insulin Resistance) is a method used to assess insulin resistance, a key feature of conditions like type 2 diabetes and metabolic syndrome. It calculates insulin resistance based on fasting blood glucose and insulin levels, providing a quick and simple estimate. HOMA-IR is commonly used in both clinical research and practice to evaluate metabolic health and monitor the effectiveness of interventions.

parameters.name: fasting_insulin
parameters.description: The fasting insulin level in uIU/mL

parameters.name: fasting_glucose
parameters.description: The fasting glucose level in mg/dL

returns.description: The estimated insulin resistance (HOMA-IR score)

scale.description: The CURB-65 score is a clinical tool used to assess the severity of pneumonia and guide decisions regarding the need for hospitalization or intensive care. It evaluates five factors: confusion, urea levels, respiratory rate, blood pressure, and age over 65 years. This scoring system helps healthcare providers determine the appropriate treatment setting, enhancing patient management and resource allocation.

parameters.name: confusion
parameters.description: Confusion status, where 0 = No and 1 = Yes

parameters.name: bun
parameters.description: Blood urea nitrogen status, where 0 = BUN ≤ 19 mg/dL (≤ 7 mmol/L urea) and 1 = BUN > 19 mg/dL (> 7 mmol/L urea)

parameters.name: respiratory_rate
parameters.description: Respiratory rate status, where 0 = < 30 breaths/min and 1 = ≥ 30 breaths/min

parameters.name: blood_pressure
parameters.description: Blood pressure status, where 0 = Systolic BP ≥ 90 mmHg and Diastolic BP > 60 mmHg, and 1 = Systolic BP < 90 mmHg or Diastolic BP ≤ 60 mmHg

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: Age status, where 0 = < 65 years and 1 = ≥ 65 years

returns.description: The CURB-65 score which ranges from 0 to 5, indicating the severity of pneumonia

scale.tool_name: PSI/PORT Score: Pneumonia Severity Index for CAP
scale.description: The Pneumonia Severity Index (PSI) or PORT Score is a clinical tool used to categorize the severity of community-acquired pneumonia (CAP) and assist in deciding the appropriate treatment setting. It evaluates factors such as age, coexisting diseases, vital signs, and laboratory findings to estimate the risk of mortality and need for hospitalization. This scoring system helps clinicians determine whether patients can be safely managed as outpatients or require more intensive inpatient care.

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: Patient's age in years

parameters.name: sex
parameters.description: Patient's sex, either 'Female' or 'Male'

parameters.name: nursing_home_resident
parameters.description: True if the patient is a nursing home resident, False otherwise

parameters.name: neoplastic_disease
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of neoplastic disease, False otherwise

parameters.name: liver_disease
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of liver disease, False otherwise

parameters.name: chf_history
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of congestive heart failure, False otherwise

parameters.name: cerebrovascular_disease
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of cerebrovascular disease, False otherwise

parameters.name: renal_disease
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of renal disease, False otherwise

parameters.name: altered_mental_status
parameters.description: True if the patient has altered mental status, False otherwise

parameters.name: respiratory_rate
parameters.description: True if respiratory rate is ≥30 breaths/min, False otherwise

parameters.name: systolic_bp
parameters.description: True if systolic blood pressure is <90 mmHg, False otherwise

parameters.name: temperature
parameters.description: True if temperature is <35°C or >39.9°C, False otherwise

parameters.name: pulse
parameters.description: True if pulse rate is ≥125 beats/min, False otherwise

parameters.name: blood_ph
parameters.description: True if blood pH is <7.35, False otherwise

parameters.name: bun
parameters.description: True if BUN is ≥30 mg/dL or ≥11 mmol/L, False otherwise

parameters.name: sodium
parameters.description: True if sodium level is <130 mmol/L, False otherwise

parameters.name: glucose
parameters.description: True if glucose level is ≥250 mg/dL or ≥14 mmol/L, False otherwise

parameters.name: hematocrit
parameters.description: True if hematocrit is <30%, False otherwise

parameters.name: oxygen_pressure
parameters.description: True if partial pressure of oxygen is <60 mmHg or <8 kPa, False otherwise

parameters.name: pleural_effusion
parameters.description: True if there is pleural effusion on x-ray, False otherwise

returns.description: The total PSI/PORT Score indicating the severity of the pneumonia

scale.description: The Child-Pugh Score is a clinical tool used to assess the prognosis of chronic liver disease, primarily cirrhosis. It evaluates the severity of cirrhosis based on five clinical measures: bilirubin, albumin levels, prothrombin time, ascites, and hepatic encephalopathy. This score helps healthcare providers determine the appropriate treatment strategy and predict patient survival.

parameters.name: bilirubin
parameters.description: Total bilirubin level in mg/dL

parameters.name: albumin
parameters.description: Albumin level in g/dL

parameters.name: inr
parameters.description: International Normalized Ratio

parameters.name: ascites
parameters.description: Describes the presence of ascites ('Absent', 'Slight', 'Moderate')

parameters.name: encephalopathy
parameters.description: Grade of hepatic encephalopathy (0 for none, 1-2 for Grade 1-2, 3-4 for Grade 3-4)

returns.description: The Child-Pugh Score, ranging from 5 (best) to 15 (worst)

scale.description: The PERC (Pulmonary Embolism Rule-out Criteria) Rule is a clinical tool used to help physicians exclude pulmonary embolism (PE) in patients deemed low-risk without resorting to advanced imaging like CT scans. It is applied in emergency settings to patients with low suspicion of PE based on initial assessment, helping to reduce unnecessary diagnostic tests and radiation exposure. The rule includes eight criteria, such as age, pulse rate, and recent surgery; if all criteria are negative, the likelihood of PE is considered sufficiently low.

parameters.name: age_over_50
parameters.description: Whether the patient is aged 50 or older. False for no, True for yes

parameters.name: heart_rate_over_100
parameters.description: Whether the patient has a heart rate of 100 bpm or higher. False for no, True for yes

parameters.name: oxygen_saturation_under_95
parameters.description: Whether the patient has an oxygen saturation less than 95%. False for no, True for yes

parameters.name: unilateral_leg_swelling
parameters.description: Whether the patient has unilateral leg swelling. False for no, True for yes

parameters.name: hemoptysis
parameters.description: Whether the patient has hemoptysis (coughing up blood). False for no, True for yes

parameters.name: recent_surgery_or_trauma
parameters.description: Whether the patient had surgery or trauma within the last 4 weeks requiring general anesthesia. False for no, True for yes

parameters.name: prior_pe_or_dvt
parameters.description: Whether the patient has a history of Pulmonary Embolism (PE) or Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT). False for no, True for yes

parameters.name: hormone_use
parameters.description: Whether the patient is using hormones such as oral contraceptives, hormone replacement therapy, or estrogenic hormones in male or female patients. False for no, True for yes

scale.description: Wells' Criteria for Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) is a clinical tool used to assess the probability of DVT in patients presenting with symptoms suggestive of this condition. It combines various clinical factors including symptoms and risk factors to generate a score that categorizes patients into low, moderate, or high probability groups. This tool is primarily utilized in outpatient settings to guide the need for further diagnostic testing such as ultrasonography or D-dimer tests.

parameters.name: active_cancer
parameters.description: True if the patient has active cancer (treatment within last 6 months)

parameters.name: bedridden_recently
parameters.description: True if the patient was bedridden for more than 3 days recently or had major surgery within the last 12 weeks

parameters.name: calf_swelling
parameters.description: True if the patient's calf swelling exceeds 3 cm compared to the other leg

parameters.name: collateral_veins
parameters.description: True if collateral non-varicose superficial veins are present

parameters.name: entire_leg_swollen
parameters.description: True if the entire leg is swollen

parameters.name: localized_tenderness
parameters.description: True if there is localized tenderness along the deep venous system

parameters.name: pitting_edema
parameters.description: True if there is pitting edema confined to the symptomatic leg

parameters.name: recent_immobilization
parameters.description: True if the patient had paralysis, paresis, or recent plaster immobilization of the lower extremities

parameters.name: previous_dvt
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of documented DVT

parameters.name: alternative_diagnosis
parameters.description: True if an alternative diagnosis is as likely or more likely than DVT

returns.description: Wells' score for DVT, where higher values indicate a higher probability of DVT

scale.tool_name: Framingham Risk Score for Hard Coronary Heart Disease
scale.description: The Framingham Risk Score for Hard Coronary Heart Disease is a tool used by healthcare professionals to estimate a patient's 10-year risk of developing severe heart disease. It utilizes factors such as age, gender, cholesterol levels, blood pressure, and smoking status to calculate risk. This score helps in identifying individuals at high risk, guiding decisions on preventive measures and interventions.

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: The age of the patient in years. Valid range: 30-79

parameters.name: sex
parameters.description: The sex of the patient. 0 for female, 1 for male

parameters.name: smoker_status
parameters.description: Smoking status of the patient. 0 for non-smoker, 1 for smoker

parameters.name: total_cholesterol
parameters.description: Total cholesterol level in mg/dL

parameters.name: hdl_cholesterol
parameters.description: HDL cholesterol level in mg/dL

parameters.name: systolic_bp
parameters.description: Systolic blood pressure in mm Hg

parameters.name: bp_medication
parameters.description: Indicates if the blood pressure is being treated with medications. 0 for no, 1 for yes

returns.description: The risk percentage of developing hard coronary heart disease

scale.tool_name: CKD-EPI Equations for Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR)
scale.description: The CKD-EPI (Chronic Kidney Disease Epidemiology Collaboration) equations are used to estimate the glomerular filtration rate (GFR), which is crucial for assessing kidney function. These equations are recommended for adults in clinical settings to detect and monitor kidney disease stages. They provide more accurate GFR estimates across different genders, ages, and races, compared to older formulas like the MDRD (Modification of Diet in Renal Disease) Study equation.

parameters.name: serum_creatinine
parameters.description: The serum creatinine level of the patient in mg/dL

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: The age of the patient in years

parameters.name: gender
parameters.description: The gender of the patient. Use 0 for male and 1 for female

returns.description: The estimated GFR in ml/min/1.73m^2

scale.description: The Caprini Score for Venous Thromboembolism (2005) is a risk assessment tool used to evaluate the likelihood of developing venous thromboembolism (VTE) in hospital patients. It is applied across various clinical settings, particularly in surgical patients, to guide the prophylactic use of anticoagulation therapy. The score incorporates multiple risk factors, including patient history, clinical conditions, and surgical factors, to stratify patients into different risk categories.

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: Patient's age in years

parameters.name: sex
parameters.description: Patient's sex (0 for male, 1 for female)

parameters.name: surgery_type
parameters.description: Type of surgery classified by duration and invasiveness (0 for none, 1 for minor, 2 for major, 5 for elective major lower extremity arthroplasty)

parameters.name: recent_event
parameters.description: Recent surgeries or events (0 for none, 1 for minor, 2 for major or laparoscopic over 45 min)

parameters.name: major_surgery
parameters.description: Whether the patient had major surgery (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: chf
parameters.description: Congestive heart failure present (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: sepsis
parameters.description: Sepsis present (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: pneumonia
parameters.description: Pneumonia present (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: plaster_cast
parameters.description: Presence of an immobilizing plaster cast (0 for no, 2 for yes)

parameters.name: fracture
parameters.description: Presence of hip, pelvis, or leg fracture (0 for no, 5 for yes)

parameters.name: stroke
parameters.description: History of stroke (0 for no, 5 for yes)

parameters.name: multiple_trauma
parameters.description: Presence of multiple traumas (0 for no, 5 for yes)

parameters.name: spinal_injury
parameters.description: Acute spinal cord injury causing paralysis (0 for no, 5 for yes)

parameters.name: varicose_veins
parameters.description: Presence of varicose veins (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: swollen_legs
parameters.description: Currently swollen legs (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: central_venous_access
parameters.description: Current central venous access (0 for no, 2 for yes)

parameters.name: history_dvt_pe
parameters.description: History of deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism (0 for no, 3 for yes)

parameters.name: family_thrombosis
parameters.description: Family history of thrombosis (0 for no, 3 for yes)

parameters.name: factor_v_leiden
parameters.description: Positive Factor V Leiden mutation (0 for no, 3 for yes)

parameters.name: prothrombin_20210A
parameters.description: Positive prothrombin 20210A mutation (0 for no, 3 for yes)

parameters.name: homocysteine
parameters.description: Elevated serum homocysteine levels (0 for no, 3 for yes)

parameters.name: lupus_anticoagulant
parameters.description: Positive lupus anticoagulant (0 for no, 3 for yes)

parameters.name: anticardiolipin_antibody
parameters.description: Elevated anticardiolipin antibody levels (0 for no, 3 for yes)

parameters.name: heparin_thrombocytopenia
parameters.description: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (0 for no, 3 for yes)

parameters.name: thrombophilia
parameters.description: Other congenital or acquired thrombophilia (0 for no, 3 for yes)

parameters.name: mobility
parameters.description: Mobility status (0 for normal, out of bed, 1 for medical patient currently on bed rest, 2 for patient confined to bed >72 hours)

parameters.name: ibd
parameters.description: History of inflammatory bowel disease (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: bmi_over_25
parameters.description: BMI over 25 (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: acute_mi
parameters.description: Acute myocardial infarction (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: copd
parameters.description: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: malignancy
parameters.description: Presence or history of malignancy (0 for no, 2 for yes)

parameters.name: other_risk_factors
parameters.description: Other unspecified risk factors (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: contraceptives_hormones
parameters.description: Use of oral contraceptives or hormone replacement therapy (0 for no, 1 for yes)

parameters.name: reproductive_history
parameters.description: History of unexplained stillborn, ≥3 spontaneous abortions, or premature birth with toxemia or growth-restricted infant (0 for no, 1 for yes)

returns.description: The calculated Caprini score

scale.tool_name: STOP-BANG Score for Obstructive Sleep Apnea
scale.description: The STOP-BANG Score is a clinical tool used to screen individuals for obstructive sleep apnea (OSA). It assesses risk factors including Snoring, Tiredness, Observed apnea, high blood Pressure, BMI, Age, Neck circumference, and Gender. This easy-to-use questionnaire is widely utilized in both primary care settings and preoperative assessments to identify patients who may need further diagnostic testing for OSA.

parameters.name: snore_loudly
parameters.description: True if the patient snores loudly (louder than talking or loud enough to be heard through closed doors), False otherwise

parameters.name: feel_tired_daytime
parameters.description: True if the patient often feels tired, fatigued, or sleepy during the daytime, False otherwise

parameters.name: observed_apnea
parameters.description: True if anyone has observed the patient stop breathing during sleep, False otherwise

parameters.name: high_blood_pressure
parameters.description: True if the patient has or is being treated for high blood pressure, False otherwise

parameters.name: bmi
parameters.description: Body Mass Index of the patient

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: Age of the patient in years

parameters.name: neck_circumference
parameters.description: Neck circumference of the patient in centimeters

parameters.name: gender
parameters.description: Gender of the patient. Expected values: 'Female' or 'Male'

returns.description: The total STOP-BANG score, ranging from 0 to 8

scale.tool_name: Gupta Perioperative Risk for Myocardial Infarction or Cardiac Arrest (MICA)
scale.description: The Gupta Perioperative Risk for Myocardial Infarction or Cardiac Arrest (MICA) score is a clinical tool used to estimate the risk of myocardial infarction or cardiac arrest in patients undergoing surgery. It incorporates factors such as patient age, type of surgery, and preexisting health conditions to provide a quantitative risk assessment. This score aids healthcare providers in making informed decisions about perioperative management and patient counseling.

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: The age of the patient in years

parameters.name: functional_status
parameters.description: Functional status of the patient

parameters.name: asa_class
parameters.description: ASA class of the patient

parameters.name: creatinine
parameters.description: Creatinine level status

parameters.name: procedure_type
parameters.description: Type of procedure being performed

returns.description: The estimated cardiac risk percentage

scale.description: A Pregnancy Due Date Calculator is used to estimate the expected date of birth, commonly known as the due date, based on the first day of the last menstrual period or conception date. It helps expectant parents and healthcare providers plan for pregnancy care and the birth. The calculator is widely used in prenatal appointments and can also be accessed online for personal tracking by pregnant individuals.

parameters.name: cycle_length
parameters.description: The length of the menstrual cycle in days. Standard cycle is 28 days

parameters.name: last_menstrual_period
parameters.description: The first day of the last menstrual period in 'YYYY/MM/DD' format

returns.description: The estimated due date in the format 'Day, Month DD, YYYY'

scale.description: Creatinine clearance, estimated using the Cockcroft-Gault equation, helps assess kidney function by estimating the rate at which the kidneys remove creatinine from the blood. This measurement is crucial for determining the proper dosing of medications that are primarily excreted by the kidneys, particularly in patients with renal impairment. It is widely used in clinical settings, especially before prescribing drugs with narrow therapeutic indices.

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: The age of the patient in years. Should be a positive integer

parameters.name: weight
parameters.description: The weight of the patient in kilograms. Should be a positive decimal

parameters.name: creatinine
parameters.description: The serum creatinine level in mg/dL. Should be a positive decimal

parameters.name: sex
parameters.description: The sex of the patient. Acceptable values are 'male' or 'female'

returns.description: The estimated creatinine clearance in mL/min

scale.description: The Corrected QT Interval (QTc) is a measure used to assess the heart's electrical recovery period, adjusted for heart rate variations. It is crucial in diagnosing conditions like Long QT Syndrome and monitoring the effects of certain medications that may prolong the QT interval, potentially leading to cardiac arrhythmias. QTc is commonly evaluated using an electrocardiogram (ECG) in clinical settings such as hospitals and health clinics.

parameters.name: qt_interval
parameters.description: The measured QT interval in milliseconds

parameters.name: heart_rate
parameters.description: The patient's heart rate in beats per minute

parameters.name: formula
parameters.description: The formula to use for correction. Options are 'Bazett', 'Fridericia',

returns.description: The corrected QT interval (QTc) in milliseconds

scale.description: Sodium correction for hyperglycemia is used to adjust the measured sodium level in the blood, which can be artificially lowered by high blood glucose levels. This correction is important in accurately diagnosing and managing electrolyte imbalances in patients with conditions such as diabetic ketoacidosis or hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state. The formula typically involves adding 1.6 mmol/L to the measured sodium level for every 100 mg/dL increase in blood glucose above normal.

parameters.name: measured_sodium
parameters.description: The measured sodium level in mEq/L

parameters.name: serum_glucose
parameters.description: The serum glucose level in mg/dL

returns.description: The corrected sodium level in mEq/L

scale.description: The Glasgow-Blatchford Bleeding Score (GBS) is a clinical tool used to assess the severity of bleeding and the need for medical intervention in patients presenting with acute upper gastrointestinal bleeding. It helps in identifying patients who may safely be managed as outpatients versus those who require hospital admission and urgent treatment. The score is based on a range of clinical and laboratory parameters, including blood pressure, hemoglobin levels, and comorbidities.

parameters.name: hemoglobin
parameters.description: Patient's hemoglobin level in g/dL

parameters.name: bun
parameters.description: Blood Urea Nitrogen (BUN) level in mg/dL

parameters.name: systolic_bp
parameters.description: Initial systolic blood pressure in mm Hg

parameters.name: sex
parameters.description: Patient's sex, 0 for female, 1 for male

parameters.name: heart_rate
parameters.description: Indicates if heart rate is ≥100 per minute, 0 for no, 1 for yes

parameters.name: melena
parameters.description: Indicates if melena is present, 0 for no, 1 for yes

parameters.name: syncope
parameters.description: Indicates if there has been a recent syncope, 0 for no, 2 for yes

parameters.name: hepatic_disease
parameters.description: Indicates history of hepatic disease, 0 for no, 2 for yes

parameters.name: cardiac_failure
parameters.description: Indicates if cardiac failure is present, 0 for no, 2 for yes

returns.description: Glasgow-Blatchford Score, ranging from 0 to 23

scale.description: The Fractional Excretion of Sodium (FENa) is a diagnostic tool used primarily to differentiate between pre-renal and intrinsic renal causes of acute kidney injury (AKI). It calculates the percentage of filtered sodium excreted by the kidneys, which helps determine if kidney dysfunction is due to decreased blood flow (pre-renal) or direct kidney damage. FENa is particularly useful in hospitalized patients with acute changes in kidney function, guiding fluid management and therapeutic interventions.

parameters.name: serum_creatinine
parameters.description: Serum creatinine concentration (mg/dL)

parameters.name: urine_sodium
parameters.description: Urine sodium concentration (mmol/L)

parameters.name: serum_sodium
parameters.description: Serum sodium concentration (mmol/L)

parameters.name: urine_creatinine
parameters.description: Urine creatinine concentration (mg/dL)

returns.description: The Fractional Excretion of Sodium (FENa) in percentage

scale.description: The Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) is a clinical tool used to assess a person's level of consciousness after a head injury. It evaluates three aspects of responsiveness: eye-opening, verbal response, and motor response, assigning a score that helps guide medical treatment. The GCS is widely used in emergency settings, intensive care units, and during the initial assessment in hospitals to monitor patient progress and prognosis.

parameters.name: eye_response
parameters.description: Score or 'NT' (not testable) for eye response

parameters.name: verbal_response
parameters.description: Score or 'NT' (not testable) for verbal response

parameters.name: motor_response
parameters.description: Score or 'NT' (not testable) for motor response

returns.description: Total GCS score if all components are testable, otherwise None

scale.tool_name: Ideal Body Weight and Adjusted Body Weight
scale.description: Ideal Body Weight (IBW) is used primarily to calculate drug dosages and medical needs where a standard weight is beneficial for clinical calculations. Adjusted Body Weight (AdjBW) is used especially in cases where patients are obese, adjusting the dosage to better match physiological needs without under- or overdosing. Both metrics are essential in healthcare settings to tailor treatments for individual patients accurately.

parameters.name: height_in_inches
parameters.description: The height of the individual in inches

parameters.name: actual_body_weight
parameters.description: The actual body weight of the individual in kilograms

parameters.name: gender
parameters.description: The gender of the individual ("male" or "female")

returns.description: A list containing the IBW and ABW in kilograms. If the individual is not obese, ABW is set equal to IBW

scale.description: The Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) Score is a medical tool used to assess the extent of organ function or rate of failure in critically ill patients. It helps predict intensive care outcomes by tracking six organ systems, including respiratory, cardiovascular, hepatic, coagulation, renal, and neurological systems. The SOFA Score is commonly used in intensive care units to evaluate a patient's status daily or to monitor changes in their condition over time.

parameters.name: pao2_fio2_ratio
parameters.description: The ratio of arterial oxygen partial pressure to fractional inspired oxygen (mmHg)

parameters.name: platelets
parameters.description: Platelet count in ×103/µL

parameters.name: glasgow_coma_scale
parameters.description: Score on the Glasgow Coma Scale

parameters.name: bilirubin
parameters.description: Serum bilirubin in mg/dL

parameters.name: map_pressure
parameters.description: Mean arterial pressure in mmHg

parameters.name: vasoactive_agents
parameters.description: Index indicating the vasoactive agents being used. Corresponds to 0 for no agents, 1 for low dose dopamine or any dose dobutamine, 2 for higher dose dopamine or low dose epinephrine/norepinephrine, 3 for very high dose of these agents

parameters.name: creatinine
parameters.description: Serum creatinine in mg/dL

parameters.name: urine_output
parameters.description: Urine output in mL/day

parameters.name: o2_delivery_type
parameters.description: Index of the type of oxygen delivery method used. Types are as follows: 0 = nasal cannula, 1 = simple face mask, 2 = non-rebreather mask, 3 = high-flow nasal cannula

parameters.name: o2_flow_rate
parameters.description: Flow rate of oxygen delivery in L/min

returns.description: Total SOFA score which is the sum of individual scores across the specified parameters."

scale.description: LDL Calculated, or LDL-C, is an estimation of the low-density lipoprotein cholesterol in the blood, often used to assess cardiovascular risk. It is commonly measured during routine health screenings or when a patient has risk factors for heart disease. This calculation helps guide decisions on lifestyle changes and medications to manage cholesterol levels effectively.

parameters.name: total_cholesterol
parameters.description: Total cholesterol level in mg/dL

parameters.name: hdl
parameters.description: High-Density Lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol level in mg/dL

parameters.name: triglycerides
parameters.description: Triglyceride level in mg/dL, measured while fasting

returns.description: Estimated LDL cholesterol level in mg/dL

scale.description: The NIH Stroke Scale/Score (NIHSS) is a tool used by healthcare professionals to objectively quantify the impairment caused by a stroke. It is administered in acute care settings, typically in emergency departments or stroke units, to assess stroke severity and guide treatment decisions. The NIHSS evaluates various neurological functions, including consciousness, vision, motor skills, and speech, helping to determine the appropriate clinical interventions.

parameters.name: loc_alertness
parameters.description: Level of consciousness - Alert (0), Arouses to minor stimulation (1), Requires repeated stimulation (2), Movements to pain (2), Postures or unresponsive (3)

parameters.name: loc_questions
parameters.description: Ask month and age - Both right (0), One right (1), None right (2), Dysarthric/intubated/trauma/language barrier (1), Aphasic (2)

parameters.name: loc_commands
parameters.description: 'Blink eyes' & 'squeeze hands' - Performs both (0), Performs one (1), Performs none (2)

parameters.name: horizontal_gaze
parameters.description: Horizontal extraocular movements - Normal (0), Partial palsy (1), Forced palsy (2)

parameters.name: visual_fields
parameters.description: Visual fields - No loss (0), Partial hemianopia (1), Complete hemianopia (2), Bilateral blindness (3), Bilateral hemianopia (3)

parameters.name: facial_palsy
parameters.description: Facial palsy - Normal (0), Minor (1), Partial (2), Unilateral complete (3), Bilateral complete (3)

parameters.name: left_arm_motor_drift
parameters.description: Left arm motor drift - No drift (0), Drift but doesn't hit bed (1), Drift, hits bed (2), Some effort against gravity (2), No effort against gravity (3), No movement (4), Amputation/joint fusion (0)

parameters.name: right_arm_motor_drift
parameters.description: Right arm motor drift - No drift (0), Drift but doesn't hit bed (1), Drift, hits bed (2), Some effort against gravity (2), No effort against gravity (3), No movement (4), Amputation/joint fusion (0)

parameters.name: left_leg_motor_drift
parameters.description: Left leg motor drift - No drift (0), Drift but doesn't hit bed (1), Drift, hits bed (2), Some effort against gravity (2), No effort against gravity (3), No movement (4), Amputation/joint fusion (0)

parameters.name: right_leg_motor_drift
parameters.description: Right leg motor drift - No drift (0), Drift but doesn't hit bed (1), Drift, hits bed (2), Some effort against gravity (2), No effort against gravity (3), No movement (4), Amputation/joint fusion (0)

parameters.name: limb_ataxia
parameters.description: Limb ataxia - No ataxia (0), Ataxia in 1 limb (1), Ataxia in 2 limbs (2), Does not understand (0), Paralyzed (0), Amputation/joint fusion (0)

parameters.name: sensation
parameters.description: Sensation - Normal (0), Mild-moderate loss (1), Mild-moderate loss (1), Complete loss (2), No response/quadriplegic (2), Coma/unresponsive (2)

parameters.name: language_aphasia
parameters.description: Language/aphasia - Normal (0), Mild-moderate aphasia (1), Severe aphasia (2), Mute/global aphasia (3), Coma/unresponsive (3)

parameters.name: dysarthria
parameters.description: Dysarthria - Normal (0), Mild-moderate (1), Severe (2), Mute/anarthric (2), Intubated/unable to test (0)

parameters.name: extinction_inattention
parameters.description: Extinction/inattention - No abnormality (0), Inattention (1), Extinction to bilateral stimulation (1), Profound hemi-inattention (2), Extinction to >1 modality (2)

returns.description: Total NIHSS score, summing all input values

scale.description: Maintenance fluids calculations are used in medical settings to determine the appropriate volume and composition of fluids required to keep a patient hydrated and maintain normal physiological functions, particularly when they cannot consume fluids orally. These calculations are essential for patients undergoing surgery, those with compromised fluid intake, and in the management of certain medical conditions like kidney diseases or severe infections. The formula considers factors such as weight, age, and clinical status to ensure the patient receives the optimal hydration needed for recovery and health maintenance.

parameters.name: weight
parameters.description: The weight of the patient in kilograms

returns.description: The calculated IV fluid rate in mL per hour

scale.description: Mean Arterial Pressure (MAP) is a critical measurement used to assess the average blood pressure in a person's arteries during one cardiac cycle. It is especially important in critical care settings to ensure adequate blood flow to organs and tissues. MAP is commonly used to monitor patients with conditions like sepsis, hypertension, or during surgery to ensure vital organs receive sufficient blood supply.

parameters.name: systolic_bp
parameters.description: Systolic blood pressure in mmHg

parameters.name: diastolic_bp
parameters.description: Diastolic blood pressure in mmHg

returns.description: The calculated mean arterial pressure in mmHg

scale.description: The MDRD (Modification of Diet in Renal Disease) GFR Equation is used to estimate glomerular filtration rate (GFR), reflecting kidney function. It is particularly useful in diagnosing and monitoring chronic kidney disease in adults, aiding in the adjustment of medication dosages and other medical management decisions. This equation is applied in clinical settings where precise kidney function assessment is crucial for patient care.

parameters.name: serum_cr
parameters.description: Serum creatinine (Cr) level in mg/dL. Should be a positive float

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: Age of the patient in years. Should be a non-negative integer

parameters.name: race_index
parameters.description: Index representing the patient's race. 0 for non-black, 1 for black

parameters.name: gender_index
parameters.description: Index representing the patient's gender. 0 for male, 1 for female

returns.description: Estimated GFR in ml/min/1.73m2

scale.description: The MELD Na (Model for End-Stage Liver Disease Sodium) score, used by the United Network for Organ Sharing (UNOS) and the Organ Procurement and Transplantation Network (OPTN), is a critical tool in prioritizing liver transplant candidates. It incorporates the patient's bilirubin, creatinine, and INR levels, along with sodium levels, to evaluate the urgency of a transplant. This score is primarily used in hospitals and transplant centers to assess the severity of chronic liver disease and allocate liver transplants more effectively.

parameters.name: creatinine
parameters.description: Serum creatinine in mg/dL. Minimum value used is 1.0. If patient's creatinine >4.0 or they received ≥2 dialysis treatments in the last 7 days or 24 hours of CVVHD, use 4.0

parameters.name: bilirubin
parameters.description: Serum bilirubin in mg/dL. Minimum value used is 1.0

parameters.name: inr
parameters.description: International Normalized Ratio (INR), unitless. Minimum value used is 1.0

parameters.name: sodium
parameters.description: Serum sodium in mEq/L. Adjusted to be within the range of 125 to 137 mEq/L

parameters.name: dialysis_treatments
parameters.description: Number of dialysis treatments in the last 7 days. Default is 0

parameters.name: cvvhd_hours
parameters.description: Hours of continuous veno-venous hemodialysis (CVVHD) within the last 7 days. Default is 0

scale.tool_name: CHA2DS2-VASc Score for Atrial Fibrillation Stroke Risk
scale.description: The CHA2DS2-VASc Score is a clinical tool used to estimate the risk of stroke in patients with atrial fibrillation. It assesses factors like congestive heart failure, hypertension, age, diabetes, stroke history, vascular disease, and sex category to calculate a score that guides anticoagulation therapy decisions. This score is commonly used in clinical settings to determine the need for initiating or adjusting anticoagulant medications to prevent stroke in these patients.

parameters.name: age
parameters.description: Age of the patient

parameters.name: sex
parameters.description: Biological sex of the patient, 'male' or 'female'

parameters.name: history_of_congestive_heart_failure
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of congestive heart failure, otherwise False

parameters.name: history_of_hypertension
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of hypertension, otherwise False

parameters.name: history_of_stroke_tia_thromboembolism
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of stroke, transient ischemic attack (TIA), or thromboembolism, otherwise False

parameters.name: history_of_vascular_disease
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of vascular disease (prior myocardial infarction, peripheral artery disease, or aortic plaque), otherwise False

parameters.name: history_of_diabetes
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of diabetes mellitus, otherwise False

returns.description: The total CHA2DS2-VASc score which is a sum of the points for each risk factor

scale.tool_name: HAS-BLED Score for Major Bleeding Risk
scale.description: The HAS-BLED score is a clinical tool used to assess the risk of major bleeding in patients with atrial fibrillation who are on anticoagulation therapy. It evaluates various risk factors, including hypertension, abnormal renal/liver function, stroke history, bleeding history or predisposition, labile INR, elderly age, drugs, or alcohol use. This score helps clinicians decide whether the benefits of anticoagulation outweigh the risks and guide adjustments in therapy.

parameters.name: hypertension
parameters.description: True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise

parameters.name: renal_disease
parameters.description: True if the patient has renal disease (dialysis, transplant, or Cr >2.26 mg/dL), False otherwise

parameters.name: liver_disease
parameters.description: True if the patient has liver disease (cirrhosis or bilirubin >2x normal with AST/ALT/AP >3x normal), False otherwise

parameters.name: stroke_history
parameters.description: True if the patient has a history of stroke, False otherwise

parameters.name: major_bleeding_history
parameters.description: True if the patient has had prior major bleeding or predisposition to bleeding, False otherwise

parameters.name: labile_inr
parameters.description: True if the patient has labile INR (unstable/high INRs, time in therapeutic range <60%), False otherwise

parameters.name: elderly
parameters.description: True if the patient is elderly (age >65), False otherwise

parameters.name: medication_risk
parameters.description: True if the patient uses medication that predisposes to bleeding (aspirin, clopidogrel, NSAIDs), False otherwise

parameters.name: alcohol_usage
parameters.description: True if the patient consumes alcohol significantly (≥8 drinks/week), False otherwise

returns.description: The total HAS-BLED score, which is a sum of all applicable risk factors

scale.description: Serum osmolality or osmolarity measures the concentration of solutes in the blood, providing insights into the body's water balance and solute concentration. It is commonly used in medical settings to assess hydration status, diagnose and manage conditions like diabetes insipidus, hyponatremia, or hypernatremia. This test helps guide treatment decisions, especially in patients presenting with altered mental status or electrolyte imbalances.

parameters.name: serum_na
parameters.description: Serum sodium concentration in mmol/L. Typical reference range is 135-145 mmol/L

parameters.name: serum_glucose
parameters.description: Serum glucose concentration in mg/dL. Typical reference range is 70-100 mg/dL

parameters.name: serum_bun
parameters.description: Blood urea nitrogen concentration in mg/dL. Typical reference range is 7-20 mg/dL

returns.description: Calculated serum osmolality in mOsm/kg
